Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Ashish, you didn't actually rebase your patch on top of this one, you submitted both this patch and your later patch as new patches, which erased the reviews and testing on this patch. At this point it would be just as good to merge them together into a single patch. 

If you want to rebase a patch onto an existing patch then you need to check out _exactly_ the same patch as the original (exactly the same commit hash) using the "checkout" button on the patch URL generator rather than "cherry-pick" the patch into your repository, and then base your patch on that checked-out original and not modify it (no rebase or similar). 

Please see "(Re-)Basing One Change on Another Change" at https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Using+Gerrit for details.

Patch Set 8:

What was the point of removing make_large_for_cancel() and other functions?

fid=$(make_large_for_cancel $f) is much more readable than      fid=$(make_custom_file_for_progress $f 103 1048576).

Stupid!

parents.subject: LU-8058 lnet: Remove old commented out code

commit.subject: LU-8203 tests: Wait for archive operation to be completed.
commit.message:
LU-8203 tests: Wait for archive operation to be completed.

As bandwith control is set to 1048576B/s in the test, so
the archive operation takes <= 40 secs to copy 39000000
bytes.

If we dont wait for the on going archive action to finish
then status will remain as STARTED and never becomes
SUCCEED as we call copytool_cleanup soon after archiving
5MB of data and the request will be stuck there for ever.

Sometimes because of previous unclean shutdown of copytool,
test_60 results in failure because of stuck request.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm,sanity-hsm

Change-Id: I2413c32a617e43fb24e7ca6696026a3c9e1cad00
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2586
Signed-off-by: Kirtankumar Krishna Shetty <kirtan.shetty@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20442
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
